gaps: NHTSA investigation and manufacturer-communication JSON routes returned Missing Authentication Token during research; use published ODI flat-file sources for those datasets.
patterns: Preserve source scope and observation timestamps.
Never derive rates without exposure denominators.

novel_features.name: Safety dossier
novel_features.description: See identity, recall and complaint summaries, and available crash ratings in one source-attributed report; add --include-records for complete source records.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining multiple NHTSA surfaces and local history while preserving scope caveats.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this instead of stitching several NHTSA responses together manually.

novel_features.name: Garage recall watch
novel_features.description: Report newly observed recall campaigns and remedy changes for saved vehicles.
novel_features.rationale: Requires first-seen and last-seen local snapshots.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for finite, auditable fleet change detection.

novel_features.name: Defect signal timeline
novel_features.description: Place complaint and recall events on one normalized timeline and identify the official flat-file sources required for investigations and communications.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-record chronology unavailable from one endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to inspect structured precursor signals without causal or rate claims.

novel_features.name: Honest model comparison
novel_features.description: Compare two model years while keeping raw complaint counts and missing denominators explicit.
novel_features.rationale: Requires normalized identities and cross-source caveats.
novel_features.example: vehicle-safety-pp-cli compare '2020 Honda Civic' '2020 Toyota Corolla' --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a defensible comparison rather than a synthetic risk score.

novel_features.name: Recall coverage reconciliation
novel_features.description: Decode one VIN, list matching model-level campaigns, and keep VIN eligibility and repair status explicitly unverified with a link to NHTSA's official lookup.
novel_features.rationale: Only a joined view can expose records present on one NHTSA surface but not the other.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when model recall eligibility and VIN open-recall status must not be conflated.

novel_features.name: Complaint-to-bulletin bridge
novel_features.description: Show structured complaint and manufacturer-communication co-occurrence candidates.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local component, vehicle, and time joins.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for auditable overlap without semantic or causal inference.

narrative.headline: Build source-attributed vehicle safety dossiers, honest comparisons, and recall-change reports from NHTSA data.
narrative.value_prop: Query NHTSA recalls, complaints, ratings, product catalogs, and VIN decoding, then preserve observations locally. Compound commands distinguish raw reports from rates and model-wide campaigns from VIN-specific open recalls.

quickstart.comment: Verify local configuration and data-source reachability.
Inspect source recall records with narrowed agent output.

troubleshoots.fix: Retry later and keep garage checks small; NHTSA prohibits bulk VIN lookup through this API.
Treat totals as raw published reports, not incidence rates; NHTSA does not provide an exposure denominator here.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI for vehicle recall, complaint, crash-rating, and VIN research that benefits from reproducible local history. Prefer its compound commands when an agent needs evidence and caveats, not merely one raw response.
anti_triggers: Do not use it for bulk commercial VIN sweeps.
Do not treat complaint counts as failure rates or the compound output as a safety certification.
Do not use it to file complaints or perform vehicle repairs.

recipes.title: Narrow a recall response
recipes.explanation: Return only campaign, component, and remedy evidence.

recipes.title: Compare two model years
recipes.command: vehicle-safety-pp-cli compare '2020 Honda Civic' '2020 Toyota Corolla' --agent
recipes.explanation: Compare source records without inventing a risk score.

recipes.title: Reconcile recall coverage
recipes.explanation: Decode the VIN and list model-level campaigns while linking to the official lookup for unverified VIN repair status.
